question_text: In a series circuit, if one of the components fails and breaks the circuit, what happens to the current in the rest of the circuit?

options.label: A
options.text: The current continues to flow normally through all other components

options.label: B
options.text: The current increases in the remaining components

options.label: C
options.text: The current stops flowing throughout the entire circuit

options.label: D
options.text: The current only flows through components nearer to the power supply

correct_answer: The current stops flowing throughout the entire circuit
explanation: In a series circuit, all components are connected in a single loop. If any component breaks or fails, it creates a gap in the circuit. Since there is only one path for current to follow, breaking this path at any point stops current flowing everywhere in the circuit. This is a key difference between series and parallel circuits.
